g.text: PDPA - Consent Withdrawal and Data Subject Rights (DSAR)
Part A - Withdraw VP consent (data already shared with a verifier) [POC-accurate]
Part B - Withdraw issuance consent (may trigger revoke) [overview]
Part C - Data Subject Rights: access / rectify / erase / restrict / portability / object (s.30-36) [overview]
A. Withdraw VP consent - data already shared with a verifier [POC-accurate]
A1. Holder requests withdrawal of a past presentation (VP consent)
Select a past presentation to withdraw
A2. Portal records the withdrawal request
id , proofId , holderOrgId , verifierOrgId , verifierName , reason
A3. Portal notifies the Verifier (data controller) via webhook
A4. Verifier acts on the withdrawal
Delete stored VP / disclosed claims for proofId
B. (overview) Withdraw ISSUANCE consent - may trigger credential revoke
Part A withdraws data already SHARED with a verifier
Part B withdraws the lawful basis for HOLDING the credential itself
is consent the ONLY basis for this credential?
C. (overview) Data Subject Rights (DSAR) - PDPA s.30-36
type = ACCESS / RECTIFY / ERASE / RESTRICT / PORTABILITY / OBJECT
[DATA STORE] Log DSAR ticket (type , requestedAt , SLA)
Provide copy of personal data + processing / audit records
[SPEC] Erasure is balanced against legal retention
minimal audit log is kept per regulatory requirement
Flag data as RESTRICTED - keep but stop active processing
Suspend credential (see Lifecycle-1) - temporarily not usable
e.g. pause processing while a rectify / object dispute is resolved
Export personal data in machine-readable format
Stop processing for the objected purpose
Lawful basis other than consent may limit erasure / withdrawal
Withdrawing consent does not affect lawfulness of processing done before withdrawal
Responses must meet the PDPA SLA (POC uses 30-day auto-approve for VP consent)
